prompt:
I inherited the sawmill cut-list branch after the contractor rolled off, and it does not build. This is everything the compiler says:

  # go-sawplan [go-sawplan.test]
  ./board.go:5:2: "strconv" imported and not used
  ./board.go:25:17: strings.ToUpper undefined (type string has no field or method ToUpper)
  ./cost.go:34:9: invalid operation: cannot take address of out[b.Species] (map index expression of struct type SpeciesTotal)
  ./cost.go:44:23: b.Boards undefined (type Board has no field or method Boards)
  ./cost.go:52:43: p.JobCost undefined (type *Plan has no field or method JobCost)
  ./plan.go:25:12: invalid operation: b.Qty * b.LengthMM (mismatched types int and int64)
  ./plan.go:60:1: missing return
  ./sawplan_test.go:122:14: p.JobCost undefined (type *Plan has no field or method JobCost)
  FAIL  go-sawplan [build failed]

As far as anyone remembers the MATH is settled — board-foot rounding, the species rollups, the waste bins — and sawplan_test.go pins all of it, so where the code is ambiguous, the tests are the spec. Please get `go test -race ./...` green with the smallest changes that keep the doc-commented behavior; don't rearrange the package and leave sawplan_test.go and go.mod alone.
notes: Six independent planted compile errors across three files: unused import (board.go), := shadowing the strings package (board.go), map-value addressability &m[k] (cost.go), wrong receiver type on JobCost — Board instead of *Plan — surfacing both in its body and at two call sites (cost.go Invoice + test), int/int64 mismatch in TotalLengthMM (plan.go), missing return on WasteBand's final path (plan.go). All eight diagnostics surface in one compile pass with go 1.26.4 — no chain reveals. The missing WasteBand arm's return value (full-rack) is pinned only by the tests, deliberately.
